proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_057 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response (i.e., feature_057 is a graded risk factor for non-response).
Patients with feature_035 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035 = 0.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rate decreases monotonically across feature_057 levels: 21.3% (level 0, n=17592), 15.5% (level 1, n=24971), 11.2% (level 2, n=7437). Logistic regression: feature_057=1 vs 0 log-OR=-0.387 (p=2e-52); feature_057=2 vs 0 log-OR=-0.764 (p=8e-77). Overall chi-square p=5.5e-98.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature_011 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of the continuous feature_006 (range 20-100; plausibly a performance-status-like score) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_099 (range ~1.7-5.5) are associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per 1-unit increase in feature_011, log-OR for response = -0.0401 (p=7.4e-35). Response rate falls from 18.6% in lowest tercile to 13.6% in highest.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per 1-unit increase in feature_006, log-OR=-0.00567 (p=1.2e-19). Response rate decreases monotonically across quartiles: 19.0% -> 17.5% -> 16.5% -> 14.7%.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per 1-unit increase in feature_099, log-OR=+0.115 (p=1.4e-6). Response rate increases across quartiles: 15.8% -> 16.8% -> 17.6% -> 17.8%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_092 (range 0.5-500) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher feature_063 (right-skewed, range 0.03-300) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_092', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per 1-unit increase in feature_092, log-OR=-0.00216 (p=1.9e-5). Response rate decreases across quartiles: 18.0% -> 17.4% -> 16.6% -> 15.6%.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_063', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per 1-unit increase in feature_063, log-OR=-0.00700 (p=2.8e-6). Effect remains highly significant on log scale: log1p(feature_063) log-OR=-0.074 (p=7e-7) after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-identified race (feature_005, 5 categories) is associated with objective_response, with white patients having the highest response rate.
Insurance category (feature_087: medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. Overall chi-square p=0.0096. Pairwise log-ORs vs asian reference are small and individually non-significant; the global association is driven mainly by 'other' and 'black' having lower rates than white. White vs non-white binary contrast: log-OR=+0.078 (p=0.0027).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_087)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. Chi-square p=0.54. No significant association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 on objective_response persists after adjusting for the strong feature_057 risk gradient (i.e., feature_035 is an independent positive prognostic marker).
The benefit of feature_035 = 1 is not modified by feature_057 (no interaction): the OR for response is similar across the three risk strata.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_035 log-OR=+0.358 (OR=1.43, p=2e-17), essentially unchanged from unadjusted estimate. Independent effect confirmed.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific log-ORs for feature_035: 0.340 in level 0, 0.367 in level 1, 0.393 in level 2. Interaction terms feature_035:C(feature_057)[T.1] p=0.75, [T.2] p=0.71. No evidence of effect-modification by cytogenetic-risk-like feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 80 binary features, after adjusting for the strongest risk factors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006), feature_093 is independently associated with higher objective_response.
Among the binary features adjusting for the same covariates, feature_014 (prevalence ~40%) is independently positively associated with response.
feature_121 (prevalence ~8%) is associated with lower probability of response after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_093 + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for feature_093 = +0.125 (OR=1.13, p=0.004). Independent positive predictor.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_014 + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for feature_014 = +0.058 (OR=1.06, p=0.017). Modest but significant positive predictor.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_121 + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for feature_121 = -0.090 (OR=0.91, p=0.044). Marginally significant negative predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on response is not significantly modified by feature_057 (i.e., the slope is similar across cytogenetic-risk-like strata).
The negative effect of feature_006 on response is not significantly modified by feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum log-ORs for feature_011: -0.041 (level 0), -0.037 (level 1), -0.052 (level 2). Interaction p-values: T.1 p=0.52, T.2 p=0.34. No strong evidence of effect-modification.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 main effect log-OR=-0.0053 (p<0.001); interaction terms feature_006:C(feature_057) T.1 p=0.82, T.2 p=0.24. Effect of feature_006 is similar across risk strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035, the white-vs-non-white contrast remains positively associated with objective_response, suggesting either residual confounding or a genuine demographic effect on response.
After adjustment for the same covariates plus age (feature_078), insurance category (feature_087) is not associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ I(feature_005=="white") + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for white vs non-white = +0.076 (OR=1.08, p=0.003). Direction and magnitude essentially unchanged from unadjusted estimate.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_087) + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035 + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-ORs for insurance categories: medicare +0.022 (p=0.55), private -0.020 (p=0.59), uninsured -0.011 (p=0.87) vs medicaid reference. None significant. Insurance is not associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92) does not have a strong main effect on objective_response after adjustment for risk factors.

Patient age modifies the cytogenetic-risk effect: among patients in the highest-risk stratum (feature_057=2), older age is associated with relatively higher response than expected (positive age x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for feature_078 (per year) = +0.00029 (p=0.79). No main effect of age after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_078 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078:C(feature_057)[T.2] interaction log-OR=+0.0085 per year (p=0.023). [T.1] interaction p=0.45. The negative effect of high feature_057 is attenuated in older patients (or equivalently the within-adverse stratum age effect is positive).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 is not modified by feature_011 (no biomarker x lab interaction).
feature_092's negative effect on response is not modified by feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * feature_035 + C(feature_057) + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_011:feature_035 log-OR=-0.014 (p=0.24). No significant effect-modification; feature_035 benefit is broadly preserved across the range of feature_011.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_092 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_092:C(feature_057)[T.1] p=0.10, [T.2] p=0.35. Slope is slightly more negative in higher-risk strata but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099's positive effect on objective_response is consistent across feature_057 strata.
Within each level of feature_057, the predictors feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006 retain the same direction of effect on response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_099 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_099 log-OR=+0.121 (p=0.001). Interaction terms p=0.95 (T.1), p=0.80 (T.2). Effect is homogeneous across risk strata.

analyses.code: for lv in [0,1,2]: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_099', data=df[df.feature_057==lv]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within-level log-ORs (feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099) — Level 0: 0.334, -0.041, -0.0052, 0.118; Level 1: 0.374, -0.037, -0.0057, 0.121; Level 2: 0.389, -0.053, -0.0081, 0.105. All key predictors retain consistent signs across strata; magnitudes broadly similar.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining the favorable cytogenetic-risk-like stratum (feature_057=0) with feature_035=1 produces an additive (or supra-additive) increase in objective response rate, with the highest response in patients positive for both.

analyses.code: df.groupby([(df.feature_057==0).astype(int)+df.feature_035])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Composite favorable score 0 (neither factor, n=30115): response 14.2%; score 1 (one factor, n=18554): 20.6%; score 2 (both, n=1331): 27.0%. Cochran-Armitage trend test p<1e-100 (Wald z>>6 from logistic regression with score). Strong dose-response gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race x feature_057 interaction is not significant: the cytogenetic-risk gradient operates similarly across racial groups.
Within the favorable feature_057=0 stratum, white patients still have the highest response rate compared with non-white groups.

analyses.code: LR test: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005) * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit() vs additive model
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio chi-square = 3.39 on 8 df, p=0.91. No evidence of interaction.

analyses.code: df[df.feature_057==0].groupby('feature_005')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=0: white 21.7%, hispanic 20.9%, black 20.5%, asian 20.4%, other 17.0%. White-vs-other contrast is small (~+1pp). Within feature_057=2: white 11.7%, hispanic 10.9%, asian 10.5%, black 9.5%, other 8.6%. Same rank order across strata, consistent with weak additive demographic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model containing the strongest predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_092, feature_063, feature_093, feature_014, race, insurance, age), feature_057 remains the dominant predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_063 + feature_093 + feature_014 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_087) + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-ORs (z-statistics in parens): feature_057=2 -0.768 (-18.6); feature_057=1 -0.384 (-15.0); feature_011 -0.040 (-12.3); feature_006 -0.0057 (-9.1); feature_035 +0.362 (+8.6); feature_099 +0.118 (+4.9); feature_063 -0.0073 (-4.8); feature_092 -0.0022 (-4.4); feature_093 +0.127 (+2.9); feature_014 +0.059 (+2.4). Insurance and age non-significant. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.019. feature_057 dominates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among other binary features tested in subgroup-specific interactions with feature_057, feature_022 (prev ~12%) shows a statistically significant overall interaction with feature_057.

analyses.code: for c in binary: LR test of feature_022 * C(feature_057)
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio chi-square=7.04 on 2 df, p=0.030. Stratum-specific log-ORs for feature_022: -0.034 (level 0, n=2131 of 17592), +0.089 (level 1), -0.236 (level 2). Marginal evidence of an interaction (uncorrected for multiple testing of 80 binary features).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063, when entered on the log scale, is a stronger and more clearly negative predictor of response than on the raw scale.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ np.log1p(feature_063) + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for log1p(feature_063) = -0.074 (z=-4.96, p=7e-7). The right-skewed predictor behaves more linearly on the log scale, consistent with a multiplicative biological effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several frequently-occurring binary features (feature_007, feature_002, feature_012, prevalences ~42-47%) have negligible main effects on objective_response and are likely demographic markers rather than treatment or biomarker indicators.

analyses.code: univariate logits for feature_007, feature_002, feature_012
analyses.result_summary: Univariate p-values: feature_007 p=0.33 (effect +0.0034 on rate), feature_002 p=0.37 (-0.003), feature_012 p=0.05 (-0.0067). All effects are tiny in magnitude (<1pp); within feature_057 strata response rates are nearly identical for 0 vs 1. Consistent with demographic/non-prognostic markers (e.g., sex).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_093's positive effect persists in joint subgroup analysis with feature_035, with no significant interaction between the two.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_093 * feature_035 + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_093 log-OR=+0.111 (p=0.014); main feature_035 log-OR=+0.344 (p<1e-14); interaction feature_093:feature_035 log-OR=+0.151 (p=0.31). Interaction trends positive (potential super-additive favorable combination) but is not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 'other' race category (smallest group, n=506) has the lowest crude response rate (14.7%), but after adjustment for cytogenetic-risk-like and lab features, this gap is no longer statistically significant.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005) + C(feature_057) + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_035', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for 'other' vs asian reference = -0.116 (p=0.26). Other coefficients: black -0.029 (p=0.64), hispanic +0.041 (p=0.49), white +0.078 (p=0.13). Race effect attenuates after adjustment — but small white-vs-non-white effect remains marginally significant (p=0.003) when collapsed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_006 (performance-status-like) is associated with lower response within every level of feature_057, supporting it as a generalizable independent prognostic factor.

analyses.code: stratified logits within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum log-ORs for feature_006 per unit: -0.0052 (lvl 0, p<0.001), -0.0057 (lvl 1, p<0.001), -0.0081 (lvl 2, p<0.001). Direction consistent and significant in all three strata; slightly larger magnitude in adverse-risk patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_011 is associated with lower response within every level of feature_057, supporting it as an independent prognostic factor.

analyses.code: stratified logits within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum log-ORs for feature_011 per unit: -0.041 (lvl 0, p<1e-15), -0.037 (lvl 1, p<1e-14), -0.053 (lvl 2, p<1e-6). Direction consistent and significant in all three strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the seven small-cardinality ordinal/categorical features (feature_018, 045, 042, 064, 096, 125, 057), only feature_057 is meaningfully associated with response.

analyses.code: chi-square tests of each small-cardinality categorical vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Chi-square p-values: feature_057 5.5e-98 (significant); feature_096 0.18; feature_064 0.49; feature_018 0.74; feature_045 0.095; feature_042 0.41; feature_125 0.073. Only feature_057 is significant; feature_045 and feature_125 show nominal trends but are not significant after correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022's stratum-specific effect within feature_057=2 (adverse) suggests a possible negative biomarker for response specifically in adverse-risk patients.

analyses.code: df[df.feature_057==2].groupby('feature_022')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within adverse-risk stratum (feature_057=2): response is 11.4% in feature_022=0 (n=6552) vs 9.3% in feature_022=1 (n=885). Stratum-specific log-OR=-0.236 (p=0.10). Direction is consistent with the broader interaction signal but the within-stratum test does not reach significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The fraction of variance in objective_response explained by all measured features is small, indicating substantial residual unexplained heterogeneity beyond the cytogenetic-like risk score and biomarkers.

analyses.code: full multivariable logit; report McFadden's pseudo-R^2 and AUC
analyses.result_summary: McFadden's pseudo-R^2 from the kitchen-sink model with all 124 features = ~0.022; from the parsimonious model (10 main predictors) = 0.019. Cytogenetic-like feature_057 alone contributes ~70% of explained pseudo-R^2. Most binary features have no detectable effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary multi-marker hypothesis: objective response is best predicted by (a) low feature_057, (b) presence of feature_035, (c) low feature_011, low feature_006, low feature_063, low feature_092, (d) high feature_099, with race, insurance and age contributing little independent predictive value.

analyses.code: fully adjusted multivariable logistic regression — directional summary
analyses.result_summary: All directions confirmed. Strongest negative predictors: feature_057 (graded), feature_011, feature_006. Strongest positive predictors: feature_035, feature_099, feature_093. Demographics (race, insurance, age) contribute negligibly after adjustment, except for a small residual white-vs-non-white advantage (log-OR ~+0.08, p=0.003). Composite favorable score (low feature_057 + feature_035=1) shows clean dose-response: 14.2% -> 20.6% -> 27.0% across 0/1/2 favorable factors.
